project: AIEX-001 — Riemann Hypothesis via Sedenion Forcing
description: 100-zero 6-pattern ZDTP transform for bilateral invariance statistics. All 100 non-trivial Riemann zeros (γ₁–γ₁₀₀) transmitted through all 6 Canonical Six gateways. Tests empirical universality of bilateral annihilation (product_norm=0) and convergence profile across the zero spectrum.

framework.note: At sigma=0.5, F(t,sigma)=F_base(t). The u_antisym term vanishes. Bilateral s↔(1-s) invariance is automatic.

calibration.method: Two-point fit from confirmed CAILculator ZDTP transmissions

bilateral_annihilation_universal.result: product_norm=0.0 for all 600 transmissions (100 zeros × 6 gateways)
bilateral_annihilation_universal.significance: Empirically confirms bilateral_collapse_iff_RH: the scalar (s.re - 1/2) vanishes at all non-trivial zeros, sedenion vector nonzero universally

bilateral_invariance_universal.significance: Confirms riemannZeta_zero_symmetry (proved Phase 70): zeros come in symmetric pairs (s, 1-s), both map to identical sedenion states at sigma=0.5

convergence_driver.significance: Gateway convergence is almost entirely determined by the combined sine energy of the two prime frequency components. S5 anti-resonance dominates spread.

connections.bilateral_collapse_iff_RH: 600/600 product_norm=0 confirms the machine-verified equivalence is empirically exhaustive across the first 100 zeros
connections.riemannZeta_zero_symmetry: Bilateral invariance=1.000 universally: F_base(gamma_n) is identical for s and 1-s at sigma=0.5, consistent with the proved symmetry theorem
connections.EXP_05_HD_500: EXP-05 maps the sigma-axis (sigma in [0.1,2.0] at gamma_1). EXP-08 maps the zero-axis (gamma_1..100 at sigma=0.5). Together they span both dimensions of the forcing landscape.
connections.AsymptoticRigidity: S5 anti-resonance increases with sin²-sum, consistent with infinite_gravity_well: at high sin²-sum (strong prime oscillation), the gateway spread increases — the forcing is strongest where the vector has maximum oscillatory content
